key_points: India has a great diversity of physical features such as mountains, plains, plateaus, deserts, coastal plains and islands
The physical features of India are divided into six major physiographic divisions
These divisions are the Himalayas, Northern Plains, Peninsular Plateau, Indian Desert, Coastal Plains and Islands
The Himalayas are the highest and youngest fold mountains in the world
The Himalayan mountain system consists of three main ranges: Himadri, Himachal and Shiwaliks
The Northern Plains were formed by the deposition of alluvial soil by the Indus, Ganga and Brahmaputra rivers
The Northern Plains are one of the most fertile and densely populated regions in India
The Peninsular Plateau is an ancient landmass made up of igneous and metamorphic rocks
The Western Ghats and Eastern Ghats form the boundaries of the Peninsular Plateau
The Indian Desert lies west of the Aravali Hills and has sand dunes and low rainfall
The coastal plains lie between the mountains and the sea
The western coastal plains lie between the Western Ghats and the Arabian Sea
The eastern coastal plains lie between the Eastern Ghats and the Bay of Bengal
India has two main island groups: Lakshadweep Islands and Andaman and Nicobar Islands
examples: Example of the highest Himalayan peak in India: Kanchenjunga
Example of a famous Himalayan valley: Kashmir Valley
Example of a river forming the Northern Plains: Ganga River
Example of a mineral rich plateau: Chotanagpur Plateau
Example of a sand dune desert: Thar Desert
Example of a western coastal region: Konkan Coast
Example of an eastern coastal region: Coromandel Coast
Example of a coral island group: Lakshadweep Islands
Example of a volcanic island: Barren Island in Andaman Sea
formulas: Physiographic divisions of India = Himalayas + Northern Plains + Peninsular Plateau + Indian Desert + Coastal Plains + Islands
Northern Plains = River deposition of Indus + Ganga + Brahmaputra
Peninsular Plateau = Ancient landmass made of igneous and metamorphic rocks
Western Coastal Plains = Western Ghats + Arabian Sea
Eastern Coastal Plains = Eastern Ghats + Bay of Bengal
exam_tips: Remember the six physiographic divisions of India
Understand the three divisions of the Himalayas: Himadri, Himachal and Shiwaliks
Learn the formation and importance of the Northern Plains
Remember that the Peninsular Plateau is the oldest landmass of India
Know the difference between Western Ghats and Eastern Ghats
Remember the location and characteristics of the Indian Desert
Learn the two island groups of India and their locations
